title: coryphopteris andreae
intro: Coryphopteris andreae is a plant species commonly known as the 'Andrea's club fern' or 'Andrea's fern'. It is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves of Coryphopteris andreae have been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
